lead.title: Warsh's First Week: No Rate Cuts This Year, Possibly Ever Under Trump's Terms
lead.standfirst: Kevin Warsh took the helm of the Federal Reserve on May 15 inheriting inflation at 3.8% and oil near $115 — both created by the president who nominated him — making the rate cuts Trump expects structurally impossible to deliver.

secondary.title: EU AI Act Omnibus: High-Risk Deadline Extended to 2027, Global Governance Race Resets
secondary.standfirst: EU legislators agreed on May 7 to push back the AI Act's high-risk compliance deadline from August 2026 to December 2027, while the Trump administration moves in the opposite direction — widening the transatlantic governance gap just as frontier models accelerate.

secondary.title: Iran War Fuel Crisis: Europe Has Weeks of Jet Fuel Left as Brent Hits $114
secondary.standfirst: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz since late February has produced what the IEA calls the largest energy crisis ever recorded — with Europe's jet fuel reserves down to weeks, and Trump's Project Freedom maritime corridor paused pending Iran talks.

secondary.title: After the Summit: Xi's Taiwan Red Line and the Contradictory Beijing Readouts
secondary.standfirst: The Trump-Xi summit produced conflicting readouts: Xi's warning that mishandling Taiwan could lead to 'clashes and even conflicts' appeared in Chinese state media but not the White House summary — and the trade deliverables the US claimed remain unverified against Chinese sources.
